skill.name: sql-injection-testing
skill.description: Comprehensive SQL injection vulnerability assessment skill for authorized penetration testing and security auditing of web applications.
skill.summary: Educational guide for SQL injection testing including detection, exploitation techniques, and remediation for security professionals.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 61 external_commands and 1 network pattern, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The backticks detected are SQL code examples in documentation markdown, not shell execution. Network references are SQL payload examples (UTL_HTTP.REQUEST), not actual network code. This is educational security documentation for authorized penetration testing with proper legal guardrails.

low_findings.title: SQL Injection Payload Documentation
low_findings.description: File contains extensive SQL injection payloads and exploitation techniques. While educational, this content requires careful handling and should only be used for authorized security testing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The entire document is a comprehensive guide to SQL injection techniques, which is appropriate for security education but requires responsible use.

content.value_statement: Web applications face constant SQL injection threats that can expose sensitive data. This skill provides systematic vulnerability assessment techniques for authorized security professionals to identify and remediate database security flaws.
actual_capabilities: Guide detection of SQL injection vulnerabilities in web application parameters
Demonstrate UNION-based, blind, and time-based extraction techniques
Document filter bypass techniques including encoding and whitespace substitution
Generate comprehensive vulnerability reports with remediation recommendations
limitations: Requires explicit written authorization before any testing activities
Does not execute actual attacks - provides educational guidance only
Cannot bypass properly implemented parameterized queries
Limited to documented techniques - may not cover novel attack vectors

use_cases.title: Penetration Testing Engagement
use_cases.description: Security consultants performing authorized assessments of client web applications can use this skill to systematically identify SQL injection vulnerabilities and demonstrate their impact.

use_cases.title: Security Code Review
use_cases.description: Development teams reviewing application code for vulnerabilities can use these techniques to understand how attackers might exploit weak input validation and implement appropriate defenses.

use_cases.title: Security Education and Training
use_cases.description: Security professionals learning about SQL injection attack vectors and defensive measures can study these documented techniques to build their expertise in web application security.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Vulnerability Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me identify potential SQL injection points in a web application. The target has a product page at /product.php?id= parameter. What are the initial test payloads I should try to detect vulnerabilities, and what responses should I look for?
prompt_templates.scenario: Initial reconnaissance for a beginner security tester

prompt_templates.title: UNION-Based Extraction Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: I've confirmed a SQL injection vulnerability exists. Guide me through determining the column count using ORDER BY, then help me construct a UNION SELECT query to extract table names from information_schema.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate exploitation technique for confirmed vulnerabilities

prompt_templates.title: Blind SQL Injection Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: The application shows no error messages and doesn't display database content. Help me design a boolean-based blind SQL injection approach to extract the database name character by character using conditional responses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced technique for applications with minimal error disclosure

prompt_templates.title: Authentication Bypass Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm testing a login form that queries: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='[input]' AND password='[input]'. Show me payloads that could bypass authentication and explain how each one transforms the resulting query.
prompt_templates.scenario: Critical vulnerability demonstration for security audit reports

output_examples.input: Test payload: ' OR '1'='1 injected into username field
output_examples.output: Authentication bypass successful. The query transforms from checking credentials to a tautology that always returns true, granting access without valid credentials.

output_examples.input: UNION SELECT NULL, table_name, NULL FROM information_schema.tables--
output_examples.output: Vulnerability report: UNION-based SQL injection confirmed. Attacker can enumerate all database tables including users, orders, payments. Severity: CRITICAL. Remediation: Implement parameterized queries.

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization defining scope and rules of engagement before testing
Use non-destructive payloads that demonstrate vulnerability without extracting real user data
Document all test cases, payloads used, and responses for comprehensive reporting
Immediately report critical findings through established emergency channels
Handle any extracted data according to data protection agreements
anti_patterns: Never execute DROP, DELETE, or TRUNCATE queries without explicit written authorization
Avoid resource-intensive queries that could cause denial of service
Do not extract more data than needed to prove the vulnerability exists
Never test systems outside the defined scope of the engagement

faq.question: Is this skill legal to use?
faq.answer: This skill is for educational purposes and authorized security testing only. You must have written permission from the system owner before testing any application you do not own. Unauthorized testing is illegal in most jurisdictions.

faq.question: Does this skill actually execute SQL injection attacks?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides educational documentation and guidance on SQL injection techniques. It does not execute any actual attacks or connect to any systems. Users must manually apply these techniques using their own tools.

faq.question: What tools do I need to perform SQL injection testing?
faq.answer: Common tools include Burp Suite for request manipulation, SQLMap for automated testing, browser developer tools, and a text editor for payload documentation. This skill guides you on how to use these tools effectively.

faq.question: How do I know if an application is vulnerable to SQL injection?
faq.answer: Look for database error messages, unusual application behavior, or response differences when injecting special characters like quotes. Boolean-based and time-based techniques can confirm vulnerabilities when errors are not displayed.

faq.question: What is the best defense against SQL injection?
faq.answer: Parameterized queries (prepared statements) are the primary defense. Additional protections include input validation, stored procedures, least-privilege database accounts, and web application firewalls as defense in depth.

faq.question: Can SQL injection be performed through HTTP headers?
faq.answer: Yes. Headers like User-Agent, Referer, and X-Forwarded-For are often logged to databases and can be injection points if not properly sanitized. Cookie values are also common injection vectors.
